title: hieracium lachenalii subsp. ausserdorferi
intro: Hieracium lachenalii subsp. ausserdorferi, a lesser-known member of the dandelion genus, is a flowering plant native to mountainous regions of Europe, characterized by its yellow dandelion-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and kidney problems due to its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Hieracium lachenalii subsp. ausserdorferi include supporting liver function, reducing blood pressure, and aiding in the removal of toxins from the body. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often dried and used in teas or infusions, or made into tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
